info.name: YouTube Live Streaming API
info.description:
The YouTube Live Streaming API lets you create, update, and manage live video broadcasts and live video streams on YouTube. The API provides resources for liveBroadcasts, liveStreams, liveChatMessages, liveChatModerators, liveChatBans, and superChatEvents. Broadcasts are scheduled events that viewers can watch live, while streams are the actual video feed bound to a broadcast.

Contact Support:
 Name: YouTube API Support

query.description: (Required) The id parameter specifies the unique ID of the broadcast that is being bound to a video stream.
(Required) Specifies a comma-separated list of one or more resource properties that the API response will include. The part parameter value must include the id property.

The streamId parameter specifies the unique ID of the video stream that is being bound to a broadcast. If this parameter is omitted, the API will remove any existing binding between the broadcast and a video stream.
API key. Your API key identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.

request.description: Binds a YouTube broadcast to a stream or removes an existing binding between a broadcast and a stream. A broadcast can only be bound to one video stream, though a video stream may be bound to more than one broadcast.

response.name: Successful response containing the updated live broadcast resource.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

request.description: Changes the status of a YouTube live broadcast and initiates any processes associated with the new status. Valid transitions are: ready -> testing, testing -> live, live -> complete, ready -> live (skips testing state).

response.name: Successful response containing the updated live broadcast resource.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

(Required) The broadcastStatus parameter identifies the state to which the broadcast is changing.
(Required) Specifies a comma-separated list of one or more resource properties that the API response will include. The part parameter value must include the id property.
API key. Your API key identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.

Comma-separated list of YouTube broadcast IDs for the resources being retrieved.
Set this parameter to true to instruct the API to return only broadcasts owned by the authenticated user.
Filters the API response to only include broadcasts with the specified status.
Filters results by the type of broadcast. Acceptable values are event and persistent.

API key. Your API key identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.
